headline: National Building Information Modelling Mandate Takes Effect
context: CIDB mandated Building Information Modelling for all government projects above RM50 million effective January 2026.

cards.big: BIM mandatory for all government projects above RM50 million from 2026
cards.sub: Digital construction planning reduces waste errors and cost overruns by 15-25%

cards.big: Only 22% of Malaysian construction firms have BIM-capable staff as of 2025
cards.sub: Training gap threatens compliance timeline for RM18 billion in government projects

cards.big: CIDB allocated RM85 million for BIM training centres in 5 states
cards.sub: Centres target training 8,000 construction professionals over 3 years

cards.big: Singapore mandated BIM in 2015 and achieved 80% industry adoption by 2022
cards.sub: Malaysian voluntary adoption since 2017 achieved only 22% suggesting mandate needed

cards.big: BIM mandate tests whether digital construction standards can transform a traditional industry
BIM mandates reduce construction waste by up to 30%. Digital standards modernise an analog industry.
